---
title: "oas"
generated: true
description: "Oracle-approximating-shrinkage (OAS) covariance estimator."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.connectivity`](../nitrix-stats-connectivity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:oas -->
```python
oas(X: Float[Array, 'n p'], *, assume_centered: bool = False) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:oas -->
Oracle-approximating-shrinkage (OAS) covariance estimator.

Uses the same convex blend of the sample covariance toward a scaled
identity as [`ledoit_wolf`](../nitrix-stats-connectivity/ledoit_wolf.qmd), but with the different closed-form
shrinkage intensity of Chen, Wiesel, Eldar and Hero (2010).  This matches
`sklearn.covariance.oas`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:oas -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `p` | — | Data matrix of `n` observations by `p` features. |
| `assume_centered` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, treat `X` as already mean-centred; otherwise centre each feature before estimating the covariance. Default `False`. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]`. The shrunk covariance matrix $\hat{\Sigma}$. The scalar shrinkage intensity $\alpha \in [0, 1]$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:oas -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
